mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing price-to-earnings multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns exceed 60% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive 60-day return direction in > 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative equity returns as growth expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day returns in > 60% of observations, or the mechanism loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 7% annualized forces aggressive central bank policy expectations, causing immediate equity sell-offs as discount rates spike and profit margins compress within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows zero incremental predictive value over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and flat UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, providing a consistent positive signal for S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward return direction, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and output gaps, triggering immediate negative equity returns as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following a > 1.0% monthly INDPRO decline during recovery phases, or the threshold signal lacks out-of-sample robustness.
